heading: Organization of Indian tribes; constitution and bylaws and amendment thereof; special election
label: 5123 Organization of Indian tribes; constitution and bylaws and amendment thereof; special election

L. 100–581“(1)‘applicable laws’ means any treaty, Executive order or Act of Congress or any final decision of the Federal courts which are applicable to the tribe, and any other laws which are applicable to the tribe pursuant to an Act of Congress or by any final decision of the Federal courts;“(2)‘appropriate tribal request’ means receipt in the Area Office of the Bureau of Indian Affairs having administrative jurisdiction over the requesting tribe, of a duly enacted tribal resolution requesting a Secretarial election as well as a copy of the proposed tribal constitution and bylaws, amendment, or revocation action;“(3)‘Secretary’ means the Secretary of the Interior.”

children.text: ratified by a majority vote of the adult members of the tribe or tribes at a special election authorized and called by the Secretary under such rules and regulations as the Secretary may prescribe; and
approved by the Secretary pursuant to subsection (d) of this section.

children.heading: Revocation
children.label: b Revocation
children.text: Any constitution or bylaws ratified and approved by the Secretary shall be revocable by an election open to the same voters and conducted in the same manner as provided in subsection (a) of this section for the adoption of a constitution or bylaws.

children.heading: Election procedure; technical assistance; review of proposals; notification of contrary-to-applicable law findings
children.label: c Election procedure; technical assistance; review of proposals; notification of contrary-to-applicable law findings
A
children.text: within one hundred and eighty days after the receipt of a tribal request for an election to ratify a proposed constitution and bylaws, or to revoke such constitution and bylaws; or

children.label: B
children.text: within ninety days after receipt of a tribal request for election to ratify an amendment to the constitution and bylaws.

children.label: A
children.text: provide such technical advice and assistance as may be requested by the tribe or as the Secretary determines may be needed; and

children.label: B
children.text: review the final draft of the constitution and bylaws, or amendments thereto to determine if any provision therein is contrary to applicable laws.

After the review provided in paragraph (2) and at least thirty days prior to the calling of the election, the Secretary shall notify the tribe, in writing, whether and in what manner the Secretary has found the proposed constitution and bylaws or amendments thereto to be contrary to applicable laws.

children.heading: Approval or disapproval by Secretary; enforcement
children.label: d Approval or disapproval by Secretary; enforcement

children.text: If an election called under subsection (a) of this section results in the adoption by the tribe of the proposed constitution and bylaws or amendments thereto, the Secretary shall approve the constitution and bylaws or amendments thereto within forty-five days after the election unless the Secretary finds that the proposed constitution and bylaws or any amendments are contrary to applicable laws.

If the Secretary does not approve or disapprove the constitution and bylaws or amendments within the forty-five days, the Secretary’s approval shall be considered as given. Actions to enforce the provisions of this section may be brought in the appropriate Federal district court.

children.heading: Vested rights and powers; advisement of presubmitted budget estimates
children.label: e Vested rights and powers; advisement of presubmitted budget estimates
children.text: In addition to all powers vested in any Indian tribe or tribal council by existing law, the constitution adopted by said tribe shall also vest in such tribe or its tribal council the following rights and powers: To employ legal counsel; to prevent the sale, disposition, lease, or encumbrance of tribal lands, interests in lands, or other tribal assets without the consent of the tribe; and to negotiate with the Federal, State, and local governments. The Secretary shall advise such tribe or its tribal council of all appropriation estimates or Federal projects for the benefit of the tribe prior to the submission of such estimates to the Office of Management and Budget and the Congress.

children.heading: Privileges and immunities of Indian tribes; prohibition on new regulations
children.label: f Privileges and immunities of Indian tribes; prohibition on new regulations
children.text: Departments or agencies of the United States shall not promulgate any regulation or make any decision or determination pursuant to the Act of ( et seq., ) as amended, or any other Act of Congress, with respect to a federally recognized Indian tribe that classifies, enhances, or diminishes the privileges and immunities available to the Indian tribe relative to other federally recognized tribes by virtue of their status as Indian tribes.June 18, 193425 U.S.C. 46148 Stat. 9841See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Privileges and immunities of Indian tribes; existing regulations
children.label: g Privileges and immunities of Indian tribes; existing regulations
children.text: Any regulation or administrative decision or determination of a department or agency of the United States that is in existence or effect on , and that classifies, enhances, or diminishes the privileges and immunities available to a federally recognized Indian tribe relative to the privileges and immunities available to other federally recognized tribes by virtue of their status as Indian tribes shall have no force or effect.May 31, 1994
each Indian tribe shall retain inherent sovereign power to adopt governing documents under procedures other than those specified in this section; and
nothing in this Act invalidates any constitution or other governing document adopted by an Indian tribe after , in accordance with the authority described in paragraph (1).June 18, 1934
